text.title: Tailwind Config, Design Tokens, @apply, and CSS Layers
text.summary: Mapping design tokens to Tailwind via CSS variables, disciplined @apply usage, CSS layer management, and dark mode setup.

section.title: Design Tokens via CSS Variables

rule.statement: Map semantic design tokens to Tailwind by using CSS custom properties (variables) in tailwind.config.js. Define the variables in @layer base. Never hardcode hex/hsl values directly in the config.
rule.rationale: CSS variables allow runtime theme switching (dark mode, white-label) without rebuilding the CSS. Hardcoded values require a rebuild for any theme change.

section.reference: Production tailwind config with CSS variable token mapping

rule.statement: Use @apply only inside @layer components for base styles that genuinely cannot be extracted to a component (e.g. .btn-base applied across multiple frameworks). Never create utility aliases with @apply.
rule.rationale: Overusing @apply defeats the utility-first model: it recreates the specificity and cascade problems that Tailwind was designed to avoid, and it increases bundle size compared to component extraction.

example.note: Utility aliases add indirection with no benefit — use the Tailwind class directly.

rule.statement: Declare @tailwind directives in order: base, components, utilities. Add custom styles only in the matching layer. Never place custom styles outside a layer — they will have unpredictable specificity.
rule.rationale: CSS layers provide explicit specificity management: utilities always override components which always override base, regardless of source order. Custom styles outside layers fall outside this guarantee.

rule.statement: Keep content paths in tailwind.config.js limited to source files only. Avoid arbitrary values like w-[347px] — define a design token instead. Limit @apply to reduce CSS bundle size.
rule.rationale: Broad content globs (e.g. **.html) scan too many files and slow builds. Arbitrary values bypass the design token system and produce one-off CSS rules that cannot be reused.
